--- title: "Hands-on_Ex0403" author: "JUNCHEN LI" date: "May 7,2026" date-modified: "last-modified" format: html editor: visual execute: eval: true echo: true warning: false freeze: auto --- ## 11.2.1 Installing and loading the packages ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} pacman::p_load(plotly, crosstalk, DT, ggdist, ggridges, colorspace, gganimate, tidyverse) ``` ::: ## 11.2.2 Data import ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} exam <- read_csv("data/Exam_data.csv") ``` ::: ## 11.3 Visualizing the uncertainty of point estimates: ggplot2 methods ::: panel-tabset ## The Ex plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
RACE n mean sd se
Chinese 193 76.50777 15.69040 1.132357
Indian 12 60.66667 23.35237 7.041005
Malay 108 57.44444 21.13478 2.043177
Others 9 69.66667 10.72381 3.791438
````` ::: ::: ## The Code ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} my_sum <- exam %>% group_by(RACE) %>% summarise( n=n(), mean=mean(MATHS), sd=sd(MATHS) ) %>% mutate(se=sd/sqrt(n-1)) knitr::kable(head(my_sum), format = 'html') ``` ::: ::: ## Self Trying ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
CLASS n mean sd se
3A 40 83.35000 6.639238 1.0631290
3B 38 80.71053 4.210077 0.6921324
3C 39 72.15385 6.788297 1.1012071
3D 38 73.86842 5.681507 0.9340339
3E 38 67.28947 8.317705 1.3674222
3F 39 62.79487 8.994675 1.4591290
````` ::: ::: ## The Code ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} my_sum1 <- exam %>% group_by(CLASS) %>% summarise( n=n(), mean=mean(ENGLISH), sd=sd(ENGLISH) ) %>% mutate(se=sd/sqrt(n-1)) knitr::kable(head(my_sum1), format = 'html') ``` ::: ::: ## 11.3.1 Plotting standard error bars of point estimates ::: Panel-tabset ## The Ex Plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex0403_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-7-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The Code ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ggplot(my_sum) + geom_errorbar( aes(x=RACE, ymin=mean-se, ymax=mean+se), width=0.2, colour="black", alpha=0.9, linewidth=0.5) + geom_point(aes (x=RACE, y=mean), stat="identity", color="red", size = 1.5, alpha=1) + ggtitle("Standard error of mean maths score by rac") ``` ::: ::: ## Self Trying ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex0403_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-9-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The Code ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ggplot(my_sum1) + geom_errorbar( aes(x=CLASS, ymin=mean-se, ymax=mean+se), width=0.2, colour="grey", alpha=0.9, linewidth=0.5) + geom_point(aes (x=CLASS, y=mean), stat="identity", color="blue", size = 1.5, alpha=1) + ggtitle("Standard error of mean maths score by CLASS") ``` ::: ::: ## 11.3.2 Plotting confidence interval of point estimates ::: panel-tabset ## The Ex plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex0403_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-11-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The Code ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ggplot(my_sum) + geom_errorbar( aes(x=reorder(RACE, -mean), ymin=mean-1.96*se, ymax=mean+1.96*se), width=0.2, colour="black", alpha=0.9, linewidth=0.5) + geom_point(aes (x=RACE, y=mean), stat="identity", color="red", size = 1.5, alpha=1) + labs(x = "Maths score", title = "95% confidence interval of mean maths score by race") ``` ::: ::: ## Self Trying ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex0403_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-13-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The Code ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ggplot(my_sum1) + geom_errorbar( aes(x=reorder(CLASS, -mean), ymin=mean-1.96*se, ymax=mean+1.96*se), width=0.2, colour="grey", alpha=0.9, linewidth=0.5) + geom_point(aes (x=CLASS, y=mean), stat="identity", color="blue", size = 1.5, alpha=1) + labs(x = "Maths score", title = "95% confidence interval of mean maths score by CLASS") ``` ::: ::: ## 11.3.3 Visualizing the uncertainty of point estimates with interactive error bars ::: panel-tabset ## The Ex plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## The Code ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} shared_df = SharedData$new(my_sum) bscols(widths = c(4,8), ggplotly((ggplot(shared_df) + geom_errorbar(aes( x=reorder(RACE, -mean), ymin=mean-2.58*se, ymax=mean+2.58*se), width=0.2, colour="black", alpha=0.9, size=0.5) + geom_point(aes( x=RACE, y=mean, text = paste("Race:", `RACE`, "
N:", `n`, "
Avg. Scores:", round(mean, digits = 2), "
95% CI:[", round((mean-2.58*se), digits = 2), ",", round((mean+2.58*se), digits = 2),"]")), stat="identity", color="red", size = 1.5, alpha=1) + xlab("Race") + ylab("Average Scores") + theme_minimal() + theme(axis.text.x = element_text( angle = 45, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)) + ggtitle("99% Confidence interval of average /
maths scores by race")), tooltip = "text"), DT::datatable(shared_df, rownames = FALSE, class="compact", width="100%", options = list(pageLength = 10, scrollX=T), colnames = c("No. of pupils", "Avg Scores", "Std Dev", "Std Error")) %>% formatRound(columns=c('mean', 'sd', 'se'), digits=2)) ``` ::: ::: ## Self Trying ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## The Code ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} shared_df = SharedData$new(my_sum1) bscols(widths = c(4,8), ggplotly((ggplot(shared_df) + geom_errorbar(aes( x=reorder(CLASS, -mean), ymin=mean-2.58*se, ymax=mean+2.58*se), width=0.2, colour="grey", alpha=0.9, size=0.5) + geom_point(aes( x=CLASS, y=mean, text = paste("CLASS:", `CLASS`, "
N:", `n`, "
Avg. Scores:", round(mean, digits = 2), "
95% CI:[", round((mean-2.58*se), digits = 2), ",", round((mean+2.58*se), digits = 2),"]")), stat="identity", color="blue", size = 1.5, alpha=1) + xlab("Class") + ylab("Average Scores") + theme_minimal() + theme(axis.text.x = element_text( angle = 45, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)) + ggtitle("99% Confidence interval of average /
maths scores by class")), tooltip = "text"), DT::datatable(shared_df, rownames = FALSE, class="compact", width="100%", options = list(pageLength = 10, scrollX=T), colnames = c("No. of pupils", "Avg Scores", "Std Dev", "Std Error")) %>% formatRound(columns=c('mean', 'sd', 'se'), digits=2)) ``` ::: ::: ## 11.4.1 Visualizing the uncertainty of point estimates: ggdist methods ::: panel-tabset ## The Ex plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex0403_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-19-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The Code ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} exam %>% ggplot(aes(x = RACE, y = MATHS)) + stat_pointinterval() + labs( title = "Visualising confidence intervals of mean math score", subtitle = "Mean Point + Multiple-interval plot") ``` ::: ::: ## Self Trying ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex0403_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-21-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The Code ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} exam %>% ggplot(aes(x = GENDER, y = SCIENCE)) + stat_pointinterval() + labs( title = "Visualising confidence intervals of mean Science score", subtitle = "Mean Point + Multiple-interval plot") ``` ::: ::: ::: panel-tabset ## The Ex plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex0403_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-23-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The Code ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} exam %>% ggplot(aes(x = RACE, y = MATHS)) + stat_pointinterval(.width = 0.95, .point = median, .interval = qi) + labs( title = "Visualising confidence intervals of median math score", subtitle = "Median Point + Multiple-interval plot") ``` ::: ::: ## Self Trying ::: panel-tabset ## The Plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex0403_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-25-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The Code ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} exam %>% ggplot(aes(x = GENDER, y = SCIENCE)) + stat_pointinterval(.width = 0.95, .point = median, .interval = qi) + labs( title = "Visualising confidence intervals of median Science score", subtitle = "Median Point + Multiple-interval plot") ``` ::: ::: ## 11.4.2 Visualizing the uncertainty of point estimates: ggdist methods ::: panel-tabset ## The Ex plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex0403_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-27-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The Code ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} exam %>% ggplot(aes(x = RACE, y = MATHS)) + stat_pointinterval( show.legend = FALSE) + labs( title = "Visualising confidence intervals of mean math score", subtitle = "Mean Point + Multiple-interval plot") ``` ::: ::: ## Self Trying ::: panel-tabset ## The Plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex0403_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-29-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The Code ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} exam %>% ggplot(aes(x = CLASS, y = ENGLISH)) + stat_pointinterval( show.legend = FALSE) + labs( title = "Visualising confidence intervals of mean English score", subtitle = "Mean Point + Multiple-interval plot") ``` ::: ::: ## 11.4.3 Visualizing the uncertainty of point estimates: ggdist methods ::: panel-tabset ## The Ex plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex0403_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-31-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The Code ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} exam %>% ggplot(aes(x = RACE, y = MATHS)) + stat_gradientinterval( fill = "skyblue", show.legend = TRUE ) + labs( title = "Visualising confidence intervals of mean math score", subtitle = "Gradient + interval plot") ``` ::: ::: ## Self Trying ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex0403_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-33-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## The Code ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} exam %>% ggplot(aes(x = CLASS, y = ENGLISH)) + stat_gradientinterval( fill = "red", show.legend = TRUE ) + labs( title = "Visualising confidence intervals of mean English score", subtitle = "Gradient + interval plot") ``` ::: ::: ## 11.5.1 Installing ungeviz package ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} devtools::install_github("wilkelab/ungeviz") ``` ::: ## 11.5.2 Launch the application in R ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(ungeviz) ``` ::: ## 11.5.3 Visualising Uncertainty with Hypothetical Outcome Plots (HOPs) ::: panel-tabset ## The Ex plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex0403_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-37-1.gif) ::: ::: ## The Code ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ggplot(data = exam, (aes(x = factor(RACE), y = MATHS))) + geom_point(position = position_jitter( height = 0.3, width = 0.05), size = 0.4, color = "#0072B2", alpha = 1/2) + geom_hpline(data = sampler(25, group = RACE), height = 0.6, color = "#D55E00") + theme_bw() + transition_states(.draw, 1, 3) ``` ::: ::: ## Self Trying ::: panel-tabset ## The plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex0403_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-39-1.gif) ::: ::: ## The Code ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ggplot(data = exam, (aes(x = factor(CLASS), y = SCIENCE))) + geom_point(position = position_jitter( height = 0.3, width = 0.05), size = 0.4, color = "#0072B2", alpha = 1/2) + geom_hpline(data = sampler(25, group = CLASS), height = 0.6, color = "#D55E00") + theme_bw() + transition_states(.draw, 1, 3) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](Hands-on_Ex0403_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-40-1.gif) ::: ::: :::
